steps.title: Use the repeated-root test
steps.content: A quadratic has exactly one unique real solution when it is a perfect square equal to $0$. That means it has one repeated root.

steps.title: Identify the perfect square
steps.content:
The expression in choice D factors as
$$x^2-14x+49=(x-7)^2$$
So the equation becomes $(x-7)^2=0$, which has only one real solution. The answer is D.
